medications.drug_name: potent topical corticosteroid (e.g. clobetasol/betamethasone dipropionate ointment)
medications.indication_rationale: Potent topical corticosteroids are first-line for localised bullous pemphigoid and reduce blistering with fewer systemic effects than oral steroids in the frail elderly.
No signs of infection; antibiotics are not indicated and risk adverse effects and resistance in a frail patient.
Reserved for extensive/refractory disease and initiated by a specialist; not needed for mild localised bullae and carries significant risk in the elderly.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Facility nurses to perform daily skin checks, keep blister roofs intact, use non-adherent dressings, and elevate the legs when seated.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Protects fragile skin, reduces secondary infection risk and eases tension.

patient_education_points.point: These are blisters from an autoimmune skin condition (bullous pemphigoid), not an infection. The immune system attacks a layer of the skin, causing fluid-filled blisters. A strong steroid ointment settles it.
Don't deliberately pop the blisters — the skin on top protects against infection. Keep the legs elevated and let staff dress them.
Tell the nurses to arrange transfer/assessment if she develops a fever, the redness spreads and becomes hot and painful, or the blister fluid turns cloudy or yellow.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to topical steroid, skin integrity/secondary infection, arrange biopsy/dermatology referral.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever, spreading hot painful redness, purulent blister fluid, or systemic decline — arrange urgent assessment/transfer.

must_include_items: Exclude cellulitis (fever/spreading warmth/pain/pus) before attributing to autoimmune blistering
Prescribe potent topical corticosteroid and protect blister roofs
Facility nurse monitoring and prompt in-person GP/dermatology review
errors_of_commission: Empirical antibiotics or ED transfer without infection signs
Starting systemic steroids remotely in a frail elderly patient
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires infection exclusion + topical steroid + monitoring/safety-net. Reflex antibiotics/ED transfer without infection signs is an over-triage/commission error.
